records.ety: [OE. burlich strong, excellent; perh. orig. fit for a lady's bower, hence handsome, manly, stout. Cf. Bower.]

defs: Having a large, strong, or gross body; stout; lusty; -- now used chiefly of human beings, but formerly of animals, in the sense of stately or beautiful, and of inanimate things that were huge and bulky.
quotes: In his latter days, with overliberal diet, [he was] somewhat corpulent and burly.
Burly and big, and studious of his ease.
It was the orator's own burly way of nonsense.
